questions.question: In BEM naming convention, what separates a Block from its Element?
questions.explanation: BEM uses double underscores (__) for elements (e.g., .card__title) and double dashes (--) for modifiers (e.g., .card--featured).

questions.question: What problem do CSS Modules solve?
options: They prevent global class name collisions by scoping styles locally to each component
They add TypeScript support to CSS
questions.answer: They prevent global class name collisions by scoping styles locally to each component
questions.explanation: CSS Modules transform class names to unique identifiers at build time (e.g., .button becomes .Button_button__3xkY8), ensuring styles don't bleed between components.

questions.question: What does the 'is-active' class prefix represent in the SMACSS methodology?
questions.explanation: In SMACSS, the 'is-' prefix (is-active, is-hidden, is-open) is reserved for State rules — styles that are toggled by JavaScript.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of the utility-first CSS approach?
options: High composability without writing new CSS for every component
questions.answer: High composability without writing new CSS for every component
questions.explanation: Utility-first CSS allows rapid UI development by combining small, single-purpose classes directly in HTML, reducing the need to create new CSS selectors for every variation.

questions.question: What is the '7-1 pattern' in CSS architecture?
options: 7 folders of Sass partials imported into 1 main file
questions.answer: 7 folders of Sass partials imported into 1 main file
questions.explanation: The 7-1 pattern organizes Sass into 7 purpose-specific folders (abstracts, base, components, layout, pages, themes, vendors) with a single main.scss that imports them all.
